question: Is the per-station value a single net tow, and is local_time_pst the tow start? Are day/night tows at the same station distinct samples?
context: We key zooscan_sample on (cruise, line, station, station_date); the data has one row per (cruise,line,station). Confirm the sampling unit and time semantics for matching to CalCOFI casts.

question: Confirm the cruise-code convention: YYYYMM + 2-letter ship code (NH=New Horizon, SR=Sally Ride, SH=Shimada, RL=Reuben Lasker, JD=David Starr Jordan, etc.).
context: We parse cruise_key (YYYY-MM-NODC) and ship_key from the code. A few codes (M2=McArthur II, MF=Miller Freeman, OS=Ocean Starr, OC=Oceanus, BH=Bold Horizon) are rarer — please confirm.

question: For the operational ZooScan classes (eggs, multiples, nauplii, others) and combined classes (cnidaria_ctenophores, pteropoda_heteropoda), are the current groupings and our WoRMS mappings appropriate?
context: 'multiples' (multiple organisms in one vignette), 'eggs', 'nauplii', 'others' have no single taxon (aphia_id NULL). Combined classes map to the higher group (Cnidaria, Gastropoda). Needed for OBIS/EDI publication.

question: What is the size-fraction / net (PRPOOS) and ZooScan processing protocol behind these abundance and biomass estimates?
context: Feret diameter (mm) and individual carbon content (ugC) are ZooScan-derived; abundance (No./m2) and C biomass (mgC/m2) are areal. Documentation of the net mesh, tow depth, and C-conversion would support cross-dataset comparison with ZooDB and euphausiids.
